questions.q: Explain the concept of horizontal analysis and its importance in the analysis of financial statements. How does it help in understanding the trend of a company's financial performance over a period of time?
questions.answer: Horizontal analysis, also known as trend analysis, is a method of comparing the financial statements of a company over a period of time to identify trends and patterns. It helps in understanding the trend of a company's financial performance over a period of time by comparing the financial statements of the current year with those of the previous years. This helps in identifying the areas of improvement and areas of concern. It is an important tool for financial analysis as it helps in identifying the changes in the financial performance of a company over time.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of horizontal analysis (1 mark)
How it helps in understanding the trend of a company's financial performance (3 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the concept of vertical analysis and its importance in the analysis of financial statements. What are the advantages of vertical analysis?
questions.answer: Vertical analysis, also known as ratio analysis, is a method of comparing the financial statements of a company with a base year or a standard to identify its financial performance. It helps in understanding the financial health of a company by comparing its financial statements with a base year or a standard. The advantages of vertical analysis include: It helps in identifying the areas of improvement and areas of concern. It provides a snapshot of a company's financial performance at a particular point of time. It helps in making comparisons with other companies in the same industry.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of vertical analysis (1 mark)

questions.q: A company's profit before tax for the year ended 31st March 2022 was Rs. 10,00,000. If the tax rate is 30%, calculate the company's profit after tax for the year.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Profit before tax = Rs. 10,00,000
Tax rate = 30%
Tax = Profit before tax x Tax rate = Rs. 10,00,000 x 30% = Rs. 3,00,000
Profit after tax = Profit before tax - Tax = Rs. 10,00,000 - Rs. 3,00,000 = Rs. 7,00,000
Therefore, the company's profit after tax for the year is Rs. 7,00,000.
marking_scheme: Calculate the profit after tax (3 marks)

questions.q: A company's total assets for the year ended 31st March 2022 were Rs. 50,00,000. If the total liabilities were Rs. 30,00,000, calculate the company's net worth for the year.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Total assets = Rs. 50,00,000
Total liabilities = Rs. 30,00,000
Net worth = Total assets - Total liabilities = Rs. 50,00,000 - Rs. 30,00,000 = Rs. 20,00,000
Therefore, the company's net worth for the year is Rs. 20,00,000.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of net worth (1 mark)

questions.q: A company's operating profit for the year ended 31st March 2022 was Rs. 5,00,000. If the interest expenses were Rs. 1,00,000 and the tax rate was 30%, calculate the company's net profit for the year.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Operating profit = Rs. 5,00,000
Interest expenses = Rs. 1,00,000
Profit before tax = Operating profit - Interest expenses = Rs. 5,00,000 - Rs. 1,00,000 = Rs. 4,00,000
Tax = Profit before tax x Tax rate = Rs. 4,00,000 x 30% = Rs. 1,20,000
Net profit = Profit before tax - Tax = Rs. 4,00,000 - Rs. 1,20,000 = Rs. 2,80,000
Therefore, the company's net profit for the year is Rs. 2,80,000.
marking_scheme: Calculate the profit before tax (2 marks)

questions.q: A company's revenue for the year ended 31st March 2022 was Rs. 20,00,000. If the cost of goods sold was Rs. 12,00,000, calculate the company's gross profit for the year.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Revenue = Rs. 20,00,000
Cost of goods sold = Rs. 12,00,000
Gross profit = Revenue - Cost of goods sold = Rs. 20,00,000 - Rs. 12,00,000 = Rs. 8,00,000
Therefore, the company's gross profit for the year is Rs. 8,00,000.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of gross profit (1 mark)
